idea-plugin.name: TabOrder
idea-plugin.description:
TabOrder — Keep your editor tabs organized, automatically

TabOrder sorts your IntelliJ editor tabs into named groups using regex rules — so you always know where each file lives, without manually dragging tabs around.

✨ Features

  • 📁 Named groups — define as many groups as you need, each with a name and sort order
  • 🔍 Regex matching — target files by path pattern (e.g. .*Test\.java$)
  • ⚡ Auto-sort on open — new tabs snap into the correct position the moment a file opens
  • 🖱 One-click reorder — right-click any tab → Order Tabs, or press Ctrl+Shift+Alt+O
  • ✅ Enable / disable groups — toggle individual rules without deleting them
  • 📦 8 built-in presets — Java/Maven, Spring Boot, Angular, Django, Laravel, Go, Kotlin/Android & full-stack Java+Angular
  • 🔄 Import / Export — share group configs across machines or teams via JSON
  • 💾 Per-project persistence — settings saved in .idea/tabGroups.xml and travel with your repo

💻 Works in all JetBrains IDEs

IntelliJ IDEA • PhpStorm • PyCharm • WebStorm • Rider • GoLand • RubyMine • DataGrip • CLion

🚀 Quick start

  1. Install from Settings → Plugins → Marketplace (search TabOrder)
  2. Open Settings → Tools → TabOrder and add your groups (or load a preset)
  3. Right-click any tab → Order Tabs — or just open a file and it sorts itself

action.description: Reorder open editor tabs according to TabOrder rules
